success_surface: Sheet-Level Column Deletion Based on Date Criteria
effective_behaviors: Handle multiple data types for date parsing (string and datetime)
Sort column deletions in reverse order to prevent index shifting
Compare both year and month for temporal filtering logic
Extract date components from timestamp strings before comparison
Validate results by verifying remaining column count and headers
Ensure output directory exists before saving files
evidence: Agent ran preliminary code to list all column headers and understand data structure
Code checked both isinstance(cell_value, str) and hasattr(cell_value, 'year') conditions
Comparison logic used: col_date.year < current_year or (col_date.year == current_year and col_date.month < current_month)
date_str = cell_value.split(' ')[0] extracted YYYY-MM-DD from full timestamp
Print statements showed total columns before/after and remaining headers
os.makedirs(path, exist_ok=True) ensured output directory existed

memory_items.title: Pre-Manipulation Structure Inspection
memory_items.lesson: Always inspect the spreadsheet structure (sheet names, row/column counts, header values) before implementing changes.
memory_items.generalization: Run diagnostic code to understand data layout, identify target ranges programmatically, and confirm expected dimensions match task requirements.

memory_items.title: Reverse-Order Batch Deletion
memory_items.lesson: When deleting multiple columns or rows, collect all targets first, sort in reverse order, then delete sequentially.
memory_items.generalization: Index shifting during batch modifications causes skipped positions; sorting descending preserves correct position tracking throughout operations.

memory_items.title: Multi-Type Data Robustness
memory_items.lesson: Handle both native datetime objects and string representations when working with date/time values.
memory_items.generalization: Check type compatibility before parsing; support string formats with consistent extraction patterns and native object properties where available.

memory_items.title: Timestamp String Normalization
memory_items.lesson: When comparing dates stored as formatted strings, extract only the relevant date component before comparison.
memory_items.generalization: Strip time portions, timezone metadata, or formatting noise from date strings prior to logical comparison operations.

memory_items.title: Temporal Filtering Precision
memory_items.lesson: For month-based comparisons, evaluate both year and month rather than year alone.
memory_items.generalization: Temporal thresholds require multi-component validation; compare all relevant temporal units to avoid boundary condition errors.

memory_items.title: Post-Manipulation Verification
memory_items.lesson: Always verify results after completing spreadsheet modifications.
memory_items.generalization: Print dimension counts and sample content after each major operation; confirm expected cells were modified and no unintended changes occurred.

memory_items.title: Output Directory Preparation
memory_items.lesson: Ensure destination directories exist before attempting to save generated files.
memory_items.generalization: Use os.makedirs with exist_ok parameter to handle missing paths gracefully without raising exceptions.

edits.content:
## 6. Advanced Batch Operations
- **Reverse-Order Deletions**: When deleting multiple columns or rows, collect all target indices first, sort in descending order, then delete sequentially to prevent index shifting.
- **Multi-Type Date Handling**: Support both native datetime objects and string representations when filtering temporal data; extract date components from timestamps before comparison.
- **Temporal Threshold Validation**: For month-based comparisons, validate both year AND month components rather than evaluating year alone.
- **Directory Preparation**: Create destination directories with `os.makedirs(path, exist_ok=True)` before saving output files.
- **Result Verification**: After manipulations, verify output dimensions (rows/columns) and spot-check key cell contents to confirm intended changes were applied correctly.
